tasks.title: Create central reference directory structure
tasks.description: Establish the .claude/references/ and .claude/templates/ directory structure with initial reference files for agents, standards, schemas, patterns, errors, and directories.
acceptance_criteria: Directory structure .claude/references/ exists with all 6 reference files
Directory structure .claude/templates/ exists with all 3 template files
All reference files follow YAML frontmatter + structured markdown format
Initial content populated based on current system analysis

tasks.title: Audit and extract duplicated agent references
tasks.description: Systematically audit all Agent-SDD files to extract agent references and create the central agents.md registry with metadata, dependencies, and usage patterns.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 core agents documented with descriptions, inputs, outputs, and usage
Inter-agent dependencies mapped and documented
Common invocation patterns identified and standardized
Agent registry validates against current system structure

tasks.title: Centralize standards references
tasks.description: Extract all references to .claude/standards/ files and create a central standards.md registry with metadata, application contexts, and validation rules.
acceptance_criteria: All 3 standards files documented with purposes and usage contexts
theme-standards.md references consolidated from 16+ files
best-practices.md and tech-stack.md references centralized
Validation rules for standards compliance defined

tasks.title: Resolve schema inconsistencies and centralize definitions
tasks.description: Resolve the 12-field vs 14-field task schema inconsistency by creating a canonical schema definition and centralizing all schema references in schemas.md.
acceptance_criteria: Single canonical task schema definition established (resolve 12-field vs 14-field)
All schema references across 8+ files unified
JSON Schema format with comprehensive validation rules
Field definitions, examples, and defaults documented
task-schema-validator.md updated to reference central schema

tasks.title: Standardize workflow patterns and error codes
tasks.description: Extract common workflow patterns and standardize error codes (ERR_001-ERR_007) into central reference files to eliminate duplication across 12+ workflow files.
acceptance_criteria: Common workflow step patterns documented and standardized
Dashboard integration patterns centralized
All error codes ERR_001-ERR_007 defined with standard messages
Error handling patterns consistent across all workflows
Validation patterns for common operations documented

tasks.title: Centralize directory structure conventions
tasks.description: Document and standardize all directory structure conventions, naming patterns, and path specifications in a central directories.md reference file.
acceptance_criteria: Spec directory naming convention standardized (.claude/specs/[create|update]-[task-id]-[date]/)
All path patterns documented with examples
Directory purposes and structures clearly defined
File naming conventions consistent across system

tasks.title: Implement reference syntax and resolution system
tasks.description: Design and implement the standardized reference syntax (<!-- REF:type:name -->) and the system for resolving references at build/validation time.
acceptance_criteria: Reference syntax defined and documented
Reference resolution mechanism implemented
Circular reference detection system in place
Automated validation of reference integrity
Version tracking for reference updates

tasks.title: Create standardized file templates
tasks.description: Develop templates for new agents, workflows, and specs that incorporate the centralized reference system and ensure consistency across new files.
acceptance_criteria: Agent template includes standard structure with reference integration
Workflow template incorporates common patterns from patterns.md
Spec template follows standardized format with schema compliance
All templates use centralized reference syntax
Templates validated against existing file structures

tasks.title: Migrate agent files to use centralized references
tasks.description: Update all agent files in .claude/agents/ to replace duplicated content with references to the centralized system, starting with the most referenced agents.
acceptance_criteria: All agent files updated to use centralized references
Duplicated content removed and replaced with REF: syntax
Agent interdependencies properly referenced
Backward compatibility maintained during transition
All agent files validate against reference system

tasks.title: Migrate workflow files to use centralized references
tasks.description: Update all workflow files in .claude/commands/workflows/ to use centralized patterns, error codes, and references instead of duplicated content.
acceptance_criteria: All workflow files use centralized error codes and patterns
Standards references consolidated using central system
Common workflow steps reference patterns.md
Error handling standardized across all workflows
Dashboard integration patterns unified

tasks.title: Implement reference validation and linting system
tasks.description: Create automated validation and linting system to ensure reference integrity, detect broken references, and validate reference syntax across all files.
acceptance_criteria: Automated reference validation system implemented
Broken reference detection and reporting
Reference syntax validation and enforcement
Pre-commit hooks for reference integrity checking
Continuous integration testing for reference system

tasks.title: Comprehensive testing and validation of new system
tasks.description: Conduct comprehensive testing of the centralized reference system including workflow validation, reference resolution testing, and performance verification.
acceptance_criteria: All workflows tested and verified to function with new system
Reference resolution performance meets requirements
No broken references or circular dependencies detected
Schema validation works correctly across all task files
System reliability verified through comprehensive testing
